author: @melristau
text:
Regarding “clockwork” motors, without an escapement mechanism, how are they “clockwork”?
Any thoughts on building an escapement mechanism to insert between the rubberband engine of a balsa airplane and its propeller? (for a more controlled release)

author: @RealityRipple-f5h
text: Should be 2303

author: @mikesmart390
text: Imagine along rod going through a flywheel with 2 bends  on the end like a crankshaft then attach two heavy duty springs attached to it once started the two springs pull against each other giving you a turning motion

author: @kke
text: Next step: pull back to wind.

author: @musicmoonshine
text: The ideal setup for it is to wind the rubber band around a shaft that's as thin as possible and as wide as possible directly, because it gives you tons and tons of rotations at low torque, the challenge is then to reduce the speed of the motor, one can do that by making a gear that lets it move and stops it intermittently, kind of like a clicker pen where if you press it a second time it comes out, when it stops it bounces and stops the unwinding of the rubber, allowing it to repeat that mechanical work and make it seem to last even longer

replies.author: @musicmoonshine
replies.text: That also gives it a clicky clicky tracktor sound

replies.author: @musicmoonshine
replies.text: This is just thinking from the perspective that fun is runtime, making the machine alternatively stop and reverse like a chameleon makes it run way longer and look weirder, it also makes it not skitter but instead drive through impulses, the chameleon like motion will make it feel more diesel and if the jitter is small enough a lot of it can be eaten up in tolerances, making the motion feel more forward and more piston like. You have to unwind the whole rubber might as well repeat little parts of it because it doesn't use fuel, all of the remaining tension is still there

replies.author: @musicmoonshine
replies.text: A plunger in corn starch liquid would also slow it down without spending tension, if you want it smooth, allowing it to move more freely when you move more slowly

author: @normandothegreat
text: Mr. Murray-Smith, cold weather is around the corner. Is there any chance of you building a heat storage device? I was hoping you would delve into a sodium acetate storage unit, maybe store the potential from a combination of solar, wind, and rocket-stove combined? 🤔 I was also wondering if the "release" could be initiated by a piezo disc in a sealed container, as well as "charged" by a glow-plug or ni-chrome element in the container as well? All my best. 😊👍

author: @stevestogsdill5791
text: Loving this series. Do you think the principle of using an escapement mechanism to manage the short burst of spring energy could be applied to a super capacitor? Rather than using resistors which reduce the efficiency of the capacitor, use a more mechanical escapement (timing circuit or a relay mechanism) to slow down the discharge without wasting power.

author: @travismoore7849
text: If you gear it lower  it will run longer and slower like a real tractor.

replies.author: @yasirrakhurrafat1142
replies.text:
But it won't run longer.
Slower sure.. but not longer.
That's how engines work as well if you think about it.
If the throttle is pressed the same amount.
The fuel consumed will be the same amount. But the lower gear will have covered less distance while the higher gear will cover more.
